final-cut-pro.name: Final Cut Pro
final-cut-pro.short_description: Apple's professional video editing software for Mac, featuring the Magnetic Timeline, ProRes support, and Apple Silicon optimization.
final-cut-pro.starting_price: $299.99 one-time purchase (Mac); Final Cut Pro for iPad is a separate subscription

features.description: A trackless, auto-rippling editing model where connected clips shift automatically as you trim or move footage, instead of requiring manual track management.
features.title: Magnetic Timeline

features.description: Synchronizes and lets you switch between multiple camera angles using audio waveforms or timecode, useful for interviews, live events, and music videos.
features.title: Multicam Editing

features.description: Color wheels, curves, and hue/saturation controls with HDR and Dolby Vision support, without needing a separate grading application.
features.title: Built-in Color Grading

features.description: Native decoding and encoding of Apple's professional codecs, with hardware acceleration on Apple Silicon Macs for smoother playback and faster exports.
features.title: ProRes and ProRes RAW Support

features.description: Titles, effects, and transitions created in the companion Motion app, or sourced from third-party designers, that drop directly into the timeline as customizable elements.
features.title: Motion Graphics Templates

features.description: AI-assisted dialogue cleanup, background noise removal, and multichannel audio mixing built directly into the editor.
features.title: Voice Isolation and Audio Tools

features.description: Machine-learning tools that reframe footage for different aspect ratios and track subjects across a shot for effects or captions.
features.title: Smart Conform and Object Tracking

features.description: Rendering, playback, and export performance tuned specifically for the media engines in M-series chips.
features.title: Apple Silicon and Metal Optimization

features.description: A separate subscription app for touch-based, on-location editing that can exchange projects with the Mac version.
features.title: Final Cut Pro for iPad Companion

pros: One-time purchase for the Mac app rather than an ongoing subscription
Magnetic Timeline speeds up assembly and trimming compared with track-based editors
Deep performance optimization for Apple Silicon Macs, including fast ProRes playback and export
Built-in color grading and audio tools reduce the need for separate specialized software
Large third-party ecosystem of plugins, effects, and Motion templates
cons: Mac-only; no Windows or Linux version, and no browser-based editing option
Project files aren't natively compatible with Premiere Pro or Avid Media Composer, complicating cross-platform collaboration
Final Cut Pro for iPad is subscription-based, creating a two-tier pricing model across devices
Fewer centralized asset-management and permission controls for large multi-editor productions compared with some competitors
Editors used to track-based timelines face a real learning curve adapting to the Magnetic Timeline paradigm

faqs.answer: The Mac version of Final Cut Pro is sold as a one-time purchase through the Mac App Store. Final Cut Pro for iPad, a separate app, is subscription-based with monthly or annual billing.
faqs.question: Is Final Cut Pro a subscription or a one-time purchase?

faqs.answer: No. Final Cut Pro is macOS-only; there is no Windows or Linux version, and there is no browser-based edition.
faqs.question: Does Final Cut Pro run on Windows?

faqs.answer: It's Final Cut Pro's trackless editing model, where clips attach to a primary storyline and connected clips automatically shift, or ripple, when you trim or move nearby footage, instead of requiring manual track management.
faqs.question: What is the Magnetic Timeline?

faqs.answer: Yes, Apple offers a free trial of Final Cut Pro for Mac, letting new users test the editing, color, and audio tools before purchasing.
faqs.question: Can I try Final Cut Pro before buying?

faqs.answer: They share editing concepts and can exchange projects, but the Mac version is a one-time purchase aimed at desktop production, while the iPad version is subscription-based and designed around touch input and on-location editing.
faqs.question: What's the difference between Final Cut Pro for Mac and Final Cut Pro for iPad?

features: Built-in color grading and HDR tools
Same feature set as the standard Mac license
Discounted pricing for eligible students and educators
Project compatibility with Final Cut Pro for Mac
Jam feature for collaborative multi-iPad editing

handbrake.name: HandBrake
handbrake.short_description: HandBrake is a free, open-source video transcoder for converting video to modern codecs like H.264, H.265, and AV1.

features.description: Encodes video with AV1, H.265, H.264, MPEG-4, MPEG-2, VP8, and VP9, into MP4, MKV, or WebM containers.
features.title: Broad codec support

features.description: Optional hardware-accelerated video encoding to speed up transcoding.
features.title: Hardware-accelerated encoding

features.description: Presets optimized for specific devices plus universal profiles for standard and high-quality conversion.
features.title: Built-in device presets

features.description: Scans and queues multiple files for batch encoding.
features.title: Batch scanning and queueing

features.description: Supports VobSub, Closed Captions (CEA-608), SSA, and SRT subtitles along with chapter markers.
features.title: Subtitle and chapter support

features.description: Encodes to AAC/HE-AAC, MP3, FLAC, AC3, E-AC3, Opus, and Vorbis, or passes through AC-3, DTS, DTS-HD, and TrueHD tracks.
features.title: Audio pass-through and encoding

features.description: Includes deinterlacing, denoise, detelecine, deblock, grayscale, cropping, and scaling filters.
features.title: Video filters

features.description: Preserves HDR metadata during conversion.
features.title: HDR pass-through

pros: Completely free, volunteer-built open-source software
Cross-platform: Windows, Mac, and Linux
Wide codec and container support, including AV1 and H.265
Batch scanning and encode queueing for processing many files at once
Hardware-accelerated encoding for faster transcodes
cons: Cannot process copy-protected DVD or Blu-ray sources
No official paid support tier
Limited to MP4, MKV, and WebM output containers

faqs.answer: Yes, HandBrake is a free, open-source tool built by volunteers.
faqs.question: Is HandBrake free?

faqs.answer: AV1, H.265, H.264, MPEG-4, MPEG-2, VP8, and VP9, output to MP4, MKV, or WebM.
faqs.question: What video codecs does HandBrake support?

faqs.answer: No, it can only process DVD or Blu-ray sources that do not contain copy protection.
faqs.question: Can HandBrake convert copy-protected discs?

faqs.answer: Yes, it supports batch scanning and an encode queue for processing multiple files.
faqs.question: Does HandBrake support batch conversion?

faqs.answer: Windows, Mac, and Linux.
faqs.question: What platforms does HandBrake run on?

founderpath.name: Founderpath

features.description: Discount rates starting from 7% for companies with $1M-$3M ARR, with funding available in as little as 24 hours.

features.description: Interest rates starting from 15% for companies with $3M+ revenue, with repayment terms of up to 4 years.
features.title: Term loans

features.description: Repayment set from 5% of monthly revenue, designed for seasonal businesses.
features.title: Merchant cash advance

features.description: Qualification is based on business data rather than a pitch deck, with fast underwriting in 24-48 hours.
features.title: Data-driven underwriting

features.description: Financing is secured by a lien on business assets only, without personal guarantees, board seats, or warrants.
features.title: No personal guarantees or board seats

features.description: Founders can repay financing early without incurring additional penalty fees.
features.title: Early repayment without penalty

pros: Non-dilutive capital that preserves founder equity
Funding decisions and disbursement can happen within 24-48 hours
No personal guarantees, board seats, or warrants required
Multiple financing structures (revenue financing, term loans, cash advances) for different business stages
4.9/5 rating on Trustpilot from over 113 verified founder reviews
cons: Term loan interest rates start at 15%, which is high relative to traditional bank financing
Requires a minimum of $500K in last-year revenue to qualify
Pricing and eligibility are underwriting-based rather than published as fixed plans
Headquarters and team size are not disclosed on the website

faqs.answer: Founderpath offers revenue financing, term loans, and merchant cash advances, each structured differently depending on company revenue and business type.
faqs.question: What types of financing does Founderpath offer?

faqs.answer: No. Founderpath uses data-driven underwriting instead of a pitch deck and provides non-dilutive capital that does not require giving up equity.
faqs.question: Does Founderpath require equity or a pitch deck?

faqs.answer: Revenue financing can be funded in as little as 24 hours after underwriting is complete.
faqs.question: How fast can I get funded?

faqs.answer: Founderpath targets software, brick-and-mortar, and CPG businesses with at least $500K in last-year revenue, healthy retention, and recurring or repeat revenue.
faqs.question: Who is eligible for Founderpath financing?

faqs.answer: No, Founderpath allows early repayment without penalty.
faqs.question: Are there penalties for paying off financing early?

features.description: Automated return preparation and on-time payment processing across US states.

kdenlive.name: Kdenlive
kdenlive.short_description: Kdenlive is a free, open-source non-linear video editor from the KDE community for Linux, Windows, macOS, and BSD.

features.description: Unlimited video and audio tracks that can be hidden or muted individually.
features.title: Unlimited multi-track editing

features.description: Handles almost any audio and video format via FFmpeg.
features.title: Broad format support

features.description: 2D title editor with animation and typewriter effects, plus Glaxnimate integration for SVG vector animation.
features.title: Built-in title editor

features.description: Dozens of artistic, blur, color-correction, and audio effects, most of which can be keyframed with multiple curve types.
features.title: Effects and filters

features.description: Simultaneous playback and editing of multiple camera angles.
features.title: Multi-cam editing

features.description: Histogram, waveform, vectorscope, and RGB parade monitoring tools.
features.title: Audio/video scopes

features.description: Automatically creates low-resolution proxy copies for smoother editing performance.
features.title: Proxy editing

features.description: Generates captions automatically using VOSK and Whisper speech models.
features.title: Automatic subtitle generation

pros: Completely free and open source, with no paid tier
Cross-platform: Linux, Windows, macOS, and BSD
Deep feature set including proxy editing, multi-cam, and keyframeable effects
Automatic subtitle generation via VOSK and Whisper
Backed by the long-established KDE community
cons: Occasional stability issues reported by users of a community-maintained open-source project
No official paid support tier for businesses

faqs.answer: Yes, Kdenlive is completely free and open source.
faqs.question: Is Kdenlive free?

faqs.answer: Linux, Windows, macOS, and BSD.
faqs.question: What platforms does Kdenlive support?

faqs.answer: Yes, it can generate captions automatically using VOSK and Whisper speech-recognition models.
faqs.question: Does Kdenlive support automatic subtitles?

faqs.answer: Yes, it can automatically create low-resolution proxy copies of footage to improve editing performance.
faqs.question: Does Kdenlive support proxy editing?
